rationale: The citing paper explicitly frames itself as extending prior evidence on large-assortment downsides, and the cited paper provides a directly relevant result that increasing recommended set size (top 50 vs top 15) worsens outcomes via deeper search in ordered environments. Given this close construct overlap (assortment size, search/choice consequences) and plausible role as a key antecedent/comparison point, this is not safely an incidental mention.
